framework.rationale: Default frontend framework. 17 screens is within React SPA comfort zone. No constraint signals override this choice.

language.rationale: Type safety is essential for a complex offline-first app with 15 entities and cross-domain data flows.

styling.rationale: Utility-first CSS framework. Aligns with tablet-first responsive design requirements and rapid UI development.

state_management.rationale: Entity count (15) exceeds 12 AND offline_first is true. Zustand with persist middleware is required for offline-first architecture -- React Context cannot persist to IndexedDB. Each domain group gets its own store for separation of concerns.

routing.rationale: Standard routing for React SPA. Supports the multi-interface navigation architecture (dashboard hub, bottom-tabs, sidebar, fullscreen).

http_client.rationale: HTTP client for server sync operations. Used only for background sync when online -- not for primary data operations which go through Dexie.

offline_storage.rationale: key_signals.storage_primary is 'indexeddb-dexie'. Dexie provides a clean API over IndexedDB for the offline-first primary data store. All 15 entities stored locally with full CRUD capability.

pwa.rationale: nfr.usability.primary_device is 'tablet' and nfr.reliability requires full offline operation. PWA enables installable app experience on tablets/smartphones with service worker caching.

printing.choice: Web Print API + thermal printer formatting
printing.rationale: nfr.integrations includes 'Thermal Receipt Printer' (required hardware integration). Using browser Print API with CSS print stylesheets formatted for 58mm/80mm thermal receipt rolls.

charting.rationale: Admin dashboard (S-008) requires revenue charts, peak hours heatmap, and payment method donut charts. Recharts is lightweight and React-native.

excel_export.rationale: Feature F-013 requires Excel export from Transaction History (S-012). SheetJS generates .xlsx files client-side without server dependency.

icons.rationale: Icon names referenced in layout.json (bar-chart-2, utensils, book-open, etc.) map directly to Lucide icon set.

framework.rationale: Default backend framework. Concurrent users (5) is low, no WebSocket requirement. Express is the sync target server -- NOT the primary data server. All core operations run offline via Dexie.

language.rationale: Shared type definitions between frontend and backend ensure data consistency during sync operations.

database.rationale: key_signals.offline_first is true. PostgreSQL serves as the cloud sync target for backup and multi-device sync -- NOT as the primary data store. Primary data lives in IndexedDB/Dexie on device.

orm.rationale: Type-safe ORM for PostgreSQL sync layer. Schema definitions serve as the canonical server-side data model.

auth.rationale: CRITICAL: key_signals.offline_first is true AND auth_hint.server_dependency is false. Auth MUST be local-first. Username/password authentication uses bcrypt hash comparison stored in IndexedDB. PIN verification for shift operations uses separate PIN hash. NO JWT -- JWT requires server availability which violates offline-first architecture. Session token stored in IndexedDB.

validation.rationale: Runtime schema validation shared between frontend (Dexie writes) and backend (sync endpoint). Ensures data integrity on both sides.

sync.choice: Custom sync layer with conflict resolution
sync.rationale: Offline-first architecture requires a sync mechanism for multi-device data consistency. Custom sync layer handles: detect online/offline, queue local changes, push to server when online, pull server updates, resolve conflicts with last-write-wins + domain-specific merge rules.

unit.rationale: Vitest integrates natively with Vite build tool for fast unit testing of stores, utilities, and sync logic.

component.rationale: Standard component testing library for React. Tests user interactions with touch-optimized components.

api.rationale: Integration testing for Express sync endpoints.

e2e.rationale: End-to-end testing for critical flows: order creation, payment processing, shift management. Supports offline mode simulation.

bundler.rationale: Fast development server and build tool. Native TypeScript and PWA plugin support.

linter.rationale: Standard JavaScript/TypeScript linting with React-specific rules.

formatter.rationale: Consistent code formatting across the project.

decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Default choice. 17 screens within SPA comfort zone. No constraints override.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Determines entire frontend ecosystem and developer requirements.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: Zustand with persist middleware instead of React Context
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Entity count (15) exceeds threshold AND offline_first requires persist-to-IndexedDB capability. React Context cannot persist state to IndexedDB. 10 domain-specific stores needed for this complexity.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: All client-side data management flows through Zustand stores backed by Dexie tables.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: Dexie.js (IndexedDB) as primary data store, PostgreSQL as sync target only
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Offline-first architecture requires all data to live on-device. IndexedDB via Dexie is the primary data store. PostgreSQL on server receives synced data for backup and multi-device access.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: All CRUD operations happen against Dexie locally. Server sync is background/opportunistic.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: Local-first authentication with bcrypt hash (NO JWT)
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: CRITICAL: offline_first is true AND auth_hint.server_dependency is false. JWT requires server availability for token issuance/refresh, which violates the offline-first requirement. Auth uses bcrypt hash comparison against credentials stored in IndexedDB. PIN verification for shift operations uses separate hash. Session managed via IndexedDB-stored session token.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Auth works entirely offline. No server round-trip needed to log in or verify PIN.

decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Low concurrent users (5). Express serves only as the sync target -- not as the primary API server. All core operations run offline via Dexie.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Backend is lightweight sync server, not a traditional REST API backend.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: Custom sync layer with last-write-wins conflict resolution
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Offline-first with multi-device sync requires a sync mechanism. Custom sync handles: online/offline detection, change queue, push when online, pull updates, conflict resolution. No existing BaaS (Firebase/Supabase) specified in constraints.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Requires building sync infrastructure: change tracking, queue, conflict resolution, and retry logic.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: PostgreSQL 16 as server-side database for sync target
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Default database choice for the sync server. Budget is flexible and timeline is medium urgency -- no signal to downgrade to SQLite.
alternatives: SQLite (lighter but less suitable for multi-device sync)
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Server infrastructure requires PostgreSQL instance for cloud backup and multi-device sync.

summary.auth_model: local-auth-hash (bcrypt, no JWT, no server dependency)
